answer: The specific couplet described — 'even the mosque's preacher's cup overflows when the wine arrives' — is not found in the Falsafa MCP corpus in that exact form. The corpus contains one work by Ghalib (Diwan-E-Ghalib, mirza-ghalib-diwan-e-ghalib-74ed4c, 239 chapters). Extensive searching across all chapters for واعظ (preacher), ملا (mullah), مسجد (mosque), پیالہ (cup), سرشار (overflowing), and related English translation terms found no couplet matching this description. The closest related couplets are: (1) Chapter 70 [p-408c0c]: 'The tavern's door, O Ghalib, and the preacher's creed? / What common ground could join them? None, we are agreed. / And yet I know, as he went in, I was just stepping out.' (Kahaan maikhane ka darwaza Ghalib aur kahaan waaiz / Par itna jaante hain kal woh jaata tha ke hum nikle) — showing the preacher entering the tavern, but no overflowing cup imagery; and (2) Chapter 130 [p-55e3af]: 'O Preacher, you can't drink it, nor can you pour a taste; / So what's the virtue of your heaven's wine, so chaste?' (Waiz na tum piyo na kisi ko pila sako / Kya baat hai tumhari sharab-e-tahoor ki). The couplet as described is not in the corpus.
